summary: Matched and linked the green shell item translation unit by switching itgshell.c from NonMatching to Matching, adjusting local code shape in several functions, adding a shared static inline shellHit helper for duplicated hit response code, and changing several itgshell-related .sdata/.sdata2 symbols from global address-style names to local labels.

key_files.role: Primary decomp source update for itgshell; changed expression shape, removed PAD_STACK uses, introduced shellHit helper, and matched four functions reported by bot.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report: 4 new matches in main/melee/it/items/itgshell

key_files.role: Build/link configuration changed itgshell.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Symbol metadata updates for local .sdata string labels and .sdata2 float/double labels used by the matched unit.

what_changed.text: configure.py now marks melee/it/items/itgshell.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
it_8028B8D8 now stores -ip->facing_dir in a temporary f32 before multiplying v.x.
it_8028BC2C and the shared hit helper compute the random upper bound through a u8 rand_max local, masking it with 0xF before HSD_Randi.
PAD_STACK(8) was removed from itGshell_UnkMotion6_Anim and from the duplicated clank/hit-shield logic when that logic was refactored.
Duplicated hit response code in itGShell_Logic14_Clanked and itGShell_Logic14_HitShield was factored into static inline void shellHit(HSD_GObj* gobj).
Several symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt were changed from global it_804D... names to local @... labels for .sdata strings and .sdata2 float/double constants.
The bot report credited four new 100% function matches: itGShell_Logic14_HitShield, it_8028BC2C, itGshell_UnkMotion6_Anim, and itGShell_Logic14_Clanked.

smart_moves.text: Factoring duplicated shell hit behavior into a static inline helper reduced repeated source while still allowing both clanked and hit-shield callbacks to match according to the bot report.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report: itGShell_Logic14_Clanked and itGShell_Logic14_HitShield 100.00%

smart_moves.text: The PR paired source-shape edits with symbol metadata updates and a configure.py status flip, keeping code matching, data/local-label ownership, and linkage status aligned.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report linked code/data increases

decomp_lessons.lesson: When two callbacks contain identical item-hit response logic, a small static inline helper can be a viable authored-source hypothesis and may improve matching without keeping duplicated generated-looking blocks.
scope: item callbacks with duplicated hit response logic
evidence_refs: raw_diff:src/melee/it/items/itgshell.c shellHit extracted from itGShell_Logic14_Clanked and reused by itGShell_Logic14_HitShield
text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report: both affected functions reached 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: For MWCC matching, preserving an intermediate local can matter even for simple expressions: this PR split -ip->facing_dir into an f32 temp before multiplying a vector component.
Splitting a masked global byte into a typed u8 local before calling HSD_Randi can be part of the matching source shape.

PAD_STACK removal can be part of final cleanup when the recovered source shape no longer needs stack padding; in this PR, removing PAD_STACK(8) coincided with reported 100% matches for affected functions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report: affected functions reached 100.00%

naming_conventions.text: The new local helper uses lower camel case shellHit, while existing exported/address-style item callback names remain unchanged.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:src/melee/it/items/itgshell.c static inline void shellHit(HSD_GObj* gobj)

naming_conventions.text: The added local variable rand_max describes the masked random range passed to HSD_Randi.
Symbol metadata for some constants/strings was changed from global it_804D... names to local @... labels, indicating local label ownership for those addresses in this PR slice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Use a temporary f32 for negated facing direction before a vector multiply.
Materialize a u8 rand_max local and apply &= 0xF before HSD_Randi instead of passing the masked expression directly.
Remove PAD_STACK(8) after source shape changes made it unnecessary for matching.
Extract repeated clank/hit-shield response into a static inline helper so both callers share identical source behavior.
Update symbol metadata from global names to local labels for affected .sdata and .sdata2 entries when matching/linking the unit.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 showed matched code +1320 bytes, linked code +5896 bytes, linked data +197 bytes, and four new matches in main/melee/it/items/itgshell.
No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/melee/it/items/itgshell.c shellHit or rand_max to see whether helper naming or typed local survived review.
Search for other item files that factor clanked and hit-shield logic into static inline helpers.
Search symbols.txt history for it_804D5270, it_804D5278, it_804DCA38, it_804DCA3C, it_804DCA40, and it_804DCA48 to confirm local label ownership remains stable.
Search for PAD_STACK removals in item matching PRs where bot reports new 100% matches.

evidence_refs: metadata_json for PR identity, state, author, timestamps, changed-file counts
raw_diff full diff for all changed files
changed_files.jsonl for file list and line counts
notes: PR body was empty and no human review comments were loaded, so reviewer intent and author rationale are not available.
The bot report provides match/link deltas and names four newly matched functions, but the slice does not include raw objdiff output or build logs.
Source-level lessons about why specific locals or inlining matched are inferred from the diff plus bot report, not from explicit author commentary.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Static inline helper for duplicated item hit logic
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2218, duplicated green shell hit response logic in itGShell_Logic14_Clanked and itGShell_Logic14_HitShield was extracted into static inline shellHit, and the bot reported both functions at 100%. Consider this as evidence that shared static inline helpers can be a viable matching shape for repeated item callback behavior when the duplicated blocks are truly identical.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report: itGShell_Logic14_Clanked and itGShell_Logic14_HitShield 100.00%

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed local for masked random bound
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2218 changed HSD_Randi(it_804D6D28->x48_byte & 0xF) into a u8 rand_max local followed by rand_max &= 0xF before HSD_Randi in it_8028BC2C and shellHit. The bot reported it_8028BC2C and the related callbacks as matched.

source_update_candidates.title: itgshell.c became Matching in PR 2218
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2218 changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itgshell.c is listed as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching). The bot report for GALE01 credited four new matches in main/melee/it/items/itgshell.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible source-owned path status update; curator should verify against current source before recording.

source_update_candidates.title: Local label ownership for itgshell-adjacent constants
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2218 changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt entries at .sdata 0x804D5270/0x804D5278 and .sdata2 0x804DCA38/0x804DCA3C/0x804DCA40/0x804DCA48 from global it_804D... symbols to local @... labels.
source_update_candidates.reason: Symbol scope/local-label facts belong in source/path metadata only after curator verifies current ownership and section context.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize shellHit lower camel case into a global naming standard; the slice only shows one helper name.
Do not claim all PAD_STACK removals are safe; this PR only supports narrow removals that coincide with reported itgshell matches.
Do not treat the symbol-label changes as standalone data ownership proof beyond the diff; current symbols and split context should be checked before promoting.
